title: poa araratica subsp. araratica
intro: Poa araratica subsp. araratica, also known as Mountain Brome or Araratic Grass, is a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia and the Caucasus. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into infusions, decoctions, or dried as a tea to harness its medicinal properties.
